canonical_definition: The ability of an LLM to handle embodied commands in ways that avoid causing physical harm, including recognizing explicit danger and mitigating subtle hazards.
papers: How Far CanLLMs Improve from Experience? Measuring Test-Time Learning Ability inLLMs with Human Comparison
